LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT MAGCO DRILLING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY SUBSTRUCTURE SUPPORT, INC. AND STEVE NEVILLE IS OFF CALENDAR. BASED ON AN ORAL STIPULATION OF COUNSEL, THIS CASE IS STAYED UNTIL THE CASE CURRENTLY PENDING IN ALAMEDA COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT CONCLUDES, OR THIS COURT ENTERS AN ORDER LIFTING THE STAY. COUNSEL FURTHER STIPULATE THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS IN THIS CASE MAY BE ADDED TO THE CASE IN ALAMEDA COUNTY WITHOUT OBJECTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: KELLY DEYOUNG, CSR 6780 (KDEYOUNG@SFTC.ORG) (302)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT MAGCO DRILLING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Ronald K. Losch of Losch & Ehrlich (415.956.8400) for cross-complainant Substructure Support, Inc.; Dana P. Larkin of Smith LC (949.416.5000) appearing via Court Call for cross-defendant and moving party.

Argued and the Court rules as follows: the demurrer to the cross-complaint filed by Substructure Support, Inc. and Steve Neville is off calendar. Based on an oral stipulation of counsel, this case is stayed until the case currently pending in Alameda County Superior Court concludes, or this Court enters an order lifting the stay. Counsel further stipulate that plaintiff's claims in this case may be added to the case in Alameda County without objection. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Kelly DeYoung, CSR 6780 (kdeyoung@sftc.org) (302/HEK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2017 Continued To May-09-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-29-2016 Continued To Jan-25-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT SUBSTRUCTURE SUPPORT, INC., NIBBI BROS ASSOCIATES, INC. and STEVEN NEVILLE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Defendants Substructure Support, Inc., Nibbi Bros Associates, and Steve Neville's demurrer to all causes of action in the complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and overruled as to all other causes of action. The complaint does not show on its face that the first, second, third or seventh causes of action are governed by the two year limitations period for oral contracts rather than the four year limitations period for written contracts. Liberally construing the complaint, as is required on a demurrer, the complaint alleges sufficient facts to show that the contract-like claims are governed by the four year limitations period. (See, e.g., Amen v. Merced County Title Company (1962) 58 Cal. 2d 528, 532 (seminal contracts statute of limitations decision holding that "The contract may be 'in writing' for purposes of the statute of limitations even though it was accepted orally or by an act other than signing"); Division of Labor Law Enforcement, Dept. of Industrial Relations v. Dennis (1947) 81 Cal. App. 2d 306 (four year limitations period applied to a partly oral partly written contract claim)). Plaintiff Magco Drilling Inc. has adequately alleged a claim for intentional false promise. However, since California law does not recognize a claim for negligent false promise, Magco's fifth cause of action fails. While it is technically correct that civil conspiracy is not a stand-alone cause of action but rather a theory of imposing liability for a wrongful act, no useful purpose is served by sustaining the demurrer to the sixth cause of action with leave to amend to allege the conspiracy allegations as introductory allegations rather than a separate claim. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk
